branches.value: Multi-path branch from N4 to N5, N6, or END

N4 -> N6 [one path selected]
N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
branches.ambiguity: The conditions imply mutually exclusive conditions, but the resolution mechanism is undefined.

validity_conditions.ambiguity: The exact threshold metric is unspecified.

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins at START and enters N1. From N1, execution branches into N2A, N2B, and/or N2C. These paths converge into N3, which proceeds directly to N4. At N4, a three-way conditional choice is made: if the threshold is not met, the process goes to N5 and loops back to N2A, N2B, and/or N2C; if one path is selected, it executes N6 and terminates at END; if continuation is not justified, it terminates immediately from N4 to END.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: START -> N1 -> {N2A, N2B, N2C} -> N3 -> N4 -> If [threshold not met] -> N5 -> {N2A, N2B, N2C}; If [one path selected] -> N6 -> END; If [continuation not justified] -> END.

ambiguities.location: N1 -> N2A / N2B / N2C and N5 -> N2A / N2B / N2C
plausible_interpretations: Parallel execution (AND split/merge) where all three sub-paths run concurrently.
Exclusive execution (XOR split/merge) where only one sub-path is chosen based on an unstated condition.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: The topological connection is clear, but the exact execution logic of the sub-paths remains structurally ambiguous.
